current_metaculus.title: What will be the status of the Arctic Metagaz before May 2026?

token_output.content:
MultipleChoiceReport(question=MultipleChoiceQuestion(question_text='What will be the status of the Arctic Metagaz before May 2026?', id_of_post=42837, page_url='https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42837', id_of_question=42778, state=, num_forecasters=0, num_predictions=0, resolution_criteria='This question will resolve as the as the status of Arctic Metagaz before May 2026, based on the following criteria: * If the ship explodes or otherwise spills the majority of its fuel into the sea, this question will immediately resolve as **Explosion/Spill**. * If the previous criterion is not met and the ship is successfully commandeered or safely neutralized this question will immediately resolve as **Ship secured**. * If the previous criteria are not met before May 2026, this question will resolve as **Ship remains adrift**.', fine_print='', background_info='The Arctic Metagaz is a Russian-flagged oil tanker that is believed to be part of Russia's [shadow fleet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russian_shadow_fleet). On March 3, 2026, the vessel, which is carrying 60,000 metric tons of liquefied natural gas, as well as 900 metric tons of diesel fuel, was [attacked by drones](https://edition.cnn.com/2026/03/16/europe/arctic-metagaz-russian-fuel-tanker-italy-malta-intl) in international waters in the Mediterranean Sea. After a fire broke out on board, the crew abandoned ship. Although the crew was rescued, by the Libyan Coast Guard, the ship has remained adrift for weeks. Alfredo Mantovano, a member of the Italian Cabinet, has [warned](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn9e9dxw3e1o) that the tanker could "explode at any moment," causing ecological devastation, and has urged the European Commission to take action. Italy has stated that it is [ready to intervene](https://gcaptain.com/two-weeks-after-explosion-arctic-metagaz-drifts-back-into-libyan-waters-amid-continued-fears-of-explosion-and-environmental-disaster/) but, as the ship is outside Italy's territorial waters, Italy is awaiting an official request from Malta or the EU. Libya and Malta have made [vague statements](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/18/world/europe/abandoned-russian-tanker-mediterranean-sea.html) about the ship, but neither has articulated a concrete plan of action.', unit_of_measure='', close_time=datetime.datetime(2026, 4, 25, 1, 0), actual_resolution_time=None, scheduled_resolution_time=datetime.datetime(2026, 5, 1, 1, 0),
